input.background: Patients with chronic renal failure undergoing haemodialysis were divided into groups with low or normal plasma heparin cofactor II (HC II) levels (Toulon et al., 1987). Five months later the acute effects of haemodialysis were studied by analysing plasma samples taken before and after haemodialysis. As dialysis increases total protein concentration in plasma, the ratio of HC II to protein was calculated, with the results shown in the following table.
input.question: Why is it wrong to conclude, as the authors did, that HC II activity increased in group 1 but not in group 2?

output.answer:
The P values associated with paired Wilcoxon tests of the data for Groups 1 and 2 are 0.01 and 0.09, which are not so far apart. More revealing are the means and standard deviations of the changes:

Group 1: Mean = -0.078, SD = 0.073
Group 2: Mean = -0.071, SD = 0.129

The mean changes in the two groups are almost the same.
